sanskrit:
ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः।
प्रोच्यते गुणसंख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि॥
transliteration:
jñānaṃ karma ca kartā ca tridhaiva guṇa-bhedataḥ |
procyate guṇa-saṅkhyāne yathāvac chṛṇu tāny api ||
literal_meaning: Knowledge, action, and the doer are declared in three ways only, according to the divisions of the qualities; hear from Me about them also, as they are in the enumeration of the qualities.
contextual_meaning: Krishna sets the stage for a triadic personality scan: every thought, deed, and sense of agency is filtered through three inner ‘operating systems’—clarity (sattva), activation (rajas), and inertia (tamas).

shadow: Fear of being reduced to a type

psychological_state: Pre-contemplative scanning—Arjuna’s mind is open but braced for a nuanced inventory of his own motives and behaviors.
trigger_scenario: A client hears ‘Let’s look at how your mind, actions, and sense of control show up in three different moods’ and leans forward, half-intrigued, half-anxious.
anchor_text: Your thoughts, deeds, and sense of agency are shaped by clarity, restlessness, or inertia—choose the mode that guides you.
